blurb: Apple's Secure Software Authorization (SSA) prevents unauthorized or downgraded firmware from running. When updating, the device submits cryptographic measurements of the software to install plus its unique ECID to Apple's authorization servers. Apple signs only the specific software bundle for that specific device, returning a personalized ticket. Because each authorization is device-specific and version-specific, it is cryptographically impossible to replay an authorization from a different device or reinstall an older, potentially vulnerable OS version after Apple has stopped signing it.
